questions.prompt: Tool fails; how to make agent resilient?

choices.text: Return error as text from tool; LLM reads + adapts. Don't crash agent. `@tool def search(q): try: return result; except Exception as e: return f'Error: {e}. Try different query.'`. LLM sees error message, decides next step (try different approach, give up, escalate). Combined with: retry with exponential backoff (tenacity) for transient failures; conditional routing for persistent errors; fallback nodes; timeouts. Production agents handle all failure modes gracefully.

questions.explanation: Return error as text; LLM adapts. Plus retry/fallback/timeout.

questions.prompt: Agent hits rate limit; crashes. Fix?

choices.text: Retry with exponential backoff. `from tenacity import retry, wait_exponential, retry_if_exception_type; @retry(wait=wait_exponential(min=4, max=60), retry=retry_if_exception_type(RateLimitError)) def call_llm(): ...`. Waits 4s, 8s, 16s, etc. up to 60s between retries. After several retries: handle as permanent failure (escalate, fallback model, defer). Standard pattern for handling rate-limited APIs.

questions.explanation: Retry with exponential backoff via tenacity. Fallback after retries exhausted.
